element_introduction_order.mastery_context: early races establish fundamental steering and acceleration on simple tracks
stunts provide acceleration boost on race tracks; critical for speed management
closed loop racing against opponents; requires sustained performance across multiple laps
dedicated stunt tracks reward points for trick execution rather than speed
environmental hazards and speed-enhancing obstacles integrated into track design
complex spatial obstacles requiring precise stunt timing and rotation control
yellow bar indicators signal optional paths; risk/reward for aggressive routing
orange/yellow bar patterns communicate upcoming dangers; visual reading skill required
bronze/silver/gold opponent tiers scale difficulty; final tour introduces Anti-Uni boss
track invisibility, reversed controls, desynchronized backgrounds; culminating challenge

typical_relief_triggers: successful stunt execution with acceleration boost
straightaway sections after complex obstacle sequences
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: fast-paced with high skill-ceiling stunt mechanics; tension modulated by obstacle density and opponent pressure; relief cycles tied to successful trick execution and speed gain

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: yellow bars indicate shortcuts; optional high-risk alternative routes communicate optional/advanced content

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tours 1-3: foundational track layouts; simple stunt opportunities; bronze-rank opponents establish baseline skill requirements
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tours 4-6: increased obstacle complexity (corkscrews, loops, twists); silver-rank opponents; multiple shortcut opportunities requiring risk assessment
difficulty_curve.late_game: Tours 7-8: gold-rank opponents; dense obstacle sequences; stunt timing becomes critical; Tour 9 (final): Anti-Uni introduces reality-distortion mechanics (invisible track, reversed controls)
notable_spikes: transition from bronze to silver rank difficulty jump
tour 4-5 introduction of complex spiral obstacles
tour 9 Anti-Uni with disorienting mechanics (control reversal, visual desynchronization)

notable_level_patterns.description: each of 9 tours contains 2 race tracks, 2 circuit tracks, and 1 stunt track; progressive difficulty through tours; rank system (bronze/silver/gold) gates progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tours 1-8 follow identical structural pattern; Tour 9 breaks pattern with Anti-Uni final boss circuit

notable_level_patterns.description: stunt execution provides speed boost on race tracks; encourages player to chain tricks; landing requires precision to avoid wipeout penalty
notable_level_patterns.example_level: any race track across all tours

notable_level_patterns.description: track composition uses bar color patterns (solid yellow = shortcut; orange/yellow = hazard ahead) to communicate terrain properties ahead; player reads track visually
early tours feature straightaways and simple jumps; mid-tours add corkscrews and multi-axis rotations; late tours combine multiple obstacles sequentially
Tour 9 final opponent introduces meta-challenge: track invisibility, control reversal, background desynchronization; fundamentally changes spatial reading and control mapping
